name: Polynices
description: Polynices, son of Oedipus and Jocasta, was a tragic figure in Greek mythology, primarily known for his role in the war of the Seven Against Thebes. Born into a cursed lineage, Polynices and his brother Eteocles agreed to alternate rule of Thebes each year. However, Eteocles refused to relinquish the throne, leading Polynices to seek aid from Adrastus, king of Argos. Adrastus gathered a formidable army, comprising seven champions, to march against Thebes and restore Polynices to his rightful position. The ensuing conflict was devastating, resulting in heavy losses on both sides and ultimately culminating in a duel between the brothers, where they mortally wounded each other. Polynices' death, along with that of Eteocles, left Thebes vulnerable and further cemented the tragic fate of the House of Oedipus. His story is a cautionary tale about ambition, betrayal, and the destructive power of familial strife, frequently depicted in Greek tragedies and influencing later artistic interpretations.
subtitle: The exiled prince of Thebes
achievements: Challenged his brother for the throne
Inspired loyalty in his Argive allies
Fought bravely in the siege of Thebes
Achieved a degree of recognition and power through alliance

keyMyths.title: The Quarrel with Eteocles
keyMyths.description: Polynices and Eteocles agreed to alternate rule of Thebes, but Eteocles broke his promise, leading to Polynices' exile and the subsequent war. This betrayal ignited the conflict that defined Polynices' life and ultimately led to his downfall.

keyMyths.title: The Seven Against Thebes
keyMyths.description: Polynices, with the aid of Adrastus, led an army of seven champions against Thebes to reclaim his throne. The siege was a catastrophic event, resulting in the death of many heroes, including Polynices and his brother.

keyMyths.title: Antigone's Defiance
keyMyths.description: After Polynices' death, Creon forbade his burial, considering him a traitor. His sister Antigone defied Creon's decree, burying Polynices and facing the consequences for her act of familial piety.

sources.author: Aeschylus
sources.description: A tragedy focusing on the siege of Thebes and the conflict between Polynices and Eteocles.
sources.text: A tragedy focusing on the siege of Thebes and the conflict between Polynices and Eteocles.

sources.author: Sophocles
sources.description: Details Oedipus's curse upon his sons and the events leading to their conflict.
